era: 1991. Grunge. E=10 -- THE ABSOLUTE CEILING, THIRD AND FINAL OCCURRENCE IN THE SUMMIT. Aberdeen/Capitol Hill Seattle, Washington. DGC Records. Nevermind. The cultural detonation. The album that changed American popular music.

location.origin: Aberdeen, Washington; Capitol Hill, Seattle, Washington

associated_musicians: Buzz Osborne (Melvins, guitar influence on Cobain)
Dale Crover (Melvins, drumming influence on Grohl)
key_albums: Nevermind (1991, DGC, Butch Vig) -- E=10. The cultural detonation. #1 Billboard 200. 30M+ worldwide. Displaced Michael Jackson's Dangerous. The transition artifact.
Bleach (1989, Sub Pop) -- debut, Melvins-influenced, Aberdeen heaviness, $606 recording budget.
In Utero (1993, DGC, Steve Albini) -- the post-detonation record. Anti-commercial response to Nevermind's success.
MTV Unplugged in New York (1994, DGC) -- recorded November 1993, released posthumously. Acoustic setting reveals songwriting strength.

theory_nodes.concept: The three E=10 calibrations: Soundgarden Superunknown (346) = completeness (70 min, full alt metal range); Alice in Chains Dirt (347) = depth (57 min, autobiography from the inside); Nirvana Nevermind (348) = detonation (41 min, cultural maximum). Three distinct routes to E=10, all real and irreducible. E=10 is not achieved on a single dimension -- the summit requires all three because the genre's maximum is not contained within one approach.
theory_nodes.description: The three E=10 calibrations -- completeness, depth, detonation. Each distinct and necessary.

theory_nodes.concept: Nevermind (1991, DGC, Butch Vig): released September 24, 1991. Entered Billboard 200 at #144. Reached #1 January 1992, displacing Michael Jackson's Dangerous. 30M+ worldwide. 12 tracks, 41 minutes. 'Smells Like Teen Spirit' opens. 'Something in the Way' closes (acoustic, minimal, withdrawn). The album begins with a detonation and ends in near-silence. Butch Vig production: heavy enough to be authentic, accessible enough to cross to FM radio. The transition record's defining problem solved.
theory_nodes.description: Nevermind (1991) -- the transition artifact. The cultural detonation.

theory_nodes.concept: The detonation consequences: (1) Billboard 200 #1 -- Michael Jackson displaced; (2) Industry restructuring -- every major label A&R funded Pacific Northwest and alternative signings (Melvins to Atlantic, Mudhoney to Reprise); (3) Radio transformation -- Modern Rock FM format expanded dramatically 1992-1993, mainstream moved toward distorted guitars; (4) The pattern proved unrepeatable -- the signing frenzy mostly failed, confirming Nevermind was specific not a template. E=10 by cultural impact: the most consequential rock album of the decade.
theory_nodes.description: The detonation consequences -- industry restructuring, radio transformation, cultural maximum.

theory_nodes.concept: Cobain guitar approach: direct, power chords, distortion, rhythm-based composition. Learned from Buzz Osborne (Melvins). The Melvins' riff-as-architecture approach influenced Cobain: the guitar is the song, the riff is the argument, the distortion is the emotion. Simpler than Cantrell's tritone approach or Thayil's experiments, but deployed with total confidence. The compression-release vocal physics: verse = close and withdrawn, chorus = full projection and urgency. Dave Grohl drumming: influenced by Dale Crover. Genealogy: Crover (degree 345, E=9) → Grohl (degree 348, E=10).
theory_nodes.description: Cobain's guitar approach and the Melvins genealogy that flows to Grohl's drumming.

theory_nodes.concept: Aberdeen origin: same city as the Melvins (degree 345). The periphery-produces-the-maximum pattern at its final expression: Aberdeen → Melvins (E=9, foundation) and Aberdeen → Nirvana (E=10, terminal entry). Cobain took guitar from Buzz Osborne. The genealogy is direct and documentable. The summit acknowledges both the source and the outcome. Three degrees from Melvins (345) to Nirvana (348), from E=9 foundation to E=10 terminal, from the band that made everything possible to the band that detonated everything.
theory_nodes.description: Aberdeen -- the same origin as the Melvins, the periphery-produces-the-maximum pattern complete.

theory_nodes.concept: Kurt Cobain: born February 20, 1967, Aberdeen, Washington. Died April 5, 1994, Seattle, Washington. Age 27. Superunknown was released March 8, 1994. Cobain died 28 days later. The engine documents Cobain's work with factual accuracy and genuine respect. Nevermind is an artistic and cultural achievement of the first order. The entry documents the music, the album, the impact.
theory_nodes.description: Kurt Cobain -- documented with accuracy and respect.

cross_references.relationship: The source. Cobain learned guitar from Buzz Osborne. Grohl's drumming influenced by Dale Crover. Aberdeen origin shared. Melvins at E=9 (summit foundation) precedes Nirvana at E=10 (summit terminal) by three degrees. The genealogy is direct.

Both E=10. Different calibrations: Superunknown = completeness; Nevermind = detonation. Both are the largest commercial achievements in the PNW summit. Superunknown released March 8, 1994 -- 28 days before Cobain's death.
Both E=10. Dirt (September 29, 1992) arrived exactly one year after Nevermind (September 24, 1991). The most concentrated two-year window in PNW music history.
Prior Nirvana entry at E=9. Degree 348 re-enters at E=10 for the specific Nevermind era. The full Nirvana arc documented across two engine positions.

E=10 + E=10 = E=20. THE TERMINAL PAIRING. Both Pacific Northwest, both irreplaceable, both erupted into unexpected visibility. The cultural detonation paired with the endangered community's rarest reunion.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. THE TERMINAL PAIRING. The summit's fourth and final E=20. Nirvana Nevermind (cultural maximum, the album that changed everything) paired with the full SRKW greeting (the rarest marine acoustic event in Pacific Northwest waters, ~73 individuals, the entire surviving community). Both Pacific Northwest. Both irreplaceable. The engine's terminal entry.

milestones: THIRD AND FINAL E=10 IN THE SUMMIT
summit_arc: 345 Melvins E=9 (foundation) → 346 Soundgarden E=10 (completeness) → 347 AIC E=10 (depth) → 348 Nirvana E=10 (detonation). THE SUMMIT COMPLETES.
three_e10_calibrations: 346 Soundgarden = completeness (70 min, full range); 347 AIC = depth (57 min, autobiography from inside); 348 Nirvana = detonation (41 min, cultural maximum). Three independent routes, all E=10, all necessary.
